profile_meta_description: Who are we? A medium-sized company from Triptis/Thuringia and module supplier for the automotive industry.  What do we do? As a supplier, we are partners to the automotive industry in the field of series processing - from development to spare parts production. Our core competence is CNC machining and module assembly of medium and large series of parts for chassis, drive and electromobility made of aluminium, cast iron and steel. profile_meta_description: At ACS Industries, we are the world leader in engineered knitted wire mesh solutions. We were founded as American Copper Sponge in Rhode Island, USA in 1939, where we manufactured knitted copper sponges.  Seven years later, we introduced our first knitted wire mesh air and grease filters.  As a result of the heavy growth in the knitted wire mesh business unit, management changed American Copper Sponge to ACS Industries, Inc. in 1960. Since the 1970s, we expanded our production into automotive exhaust products, and introduced our first knitted wire mesh support systems.   Today, we are the world’s largest vertically integrated knitted wire mesh manufacturer. Additionally, we are a key global supplier of pyrotechnic airbag inflator filters and exhaust systems components to both Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs) and Tier 1 suppliers. Furthermore, we are a global supplier to both the foodservice and janitorial industries.

profile_meta_description: For more than 20 years, Graebener® has been one of the first companies to focus on the research and development of manufacturing processes and machines for fuel cell and electrolyzer components.  The company has dedicated itself to setting the quality standard for manufacturing technologies by developing innovative processes and machines that help to manufacture components, such as the metallic bipolar plate, that are tailor-made, integrable, scalable and thus economically efficient.  For this purpose, Graebener® accompanies all strategically important steps towards the optimally designed production line: Starting from engineering with a view to plate design and line concept via prototyping and small series production in the in-house production laboratory up to the customized production machine or line.   Experience from more than 100 years of metal processing and worldwide customer-specific special solutions are incorporated into the holistic development partnership with the customer.

profile_meta_description: Logstrup is a global supplier of low-voltage switchgear and controlgear systems with more than 65 years of experience. Since 1958, we have specialized in modular, type-tested switchboards and Motor Control Centre (MCC) solutions for critical and demanding applications.  Our IEC 61439-1/-2 compliant systems ensure safe, flexible, and scalable power distribution – from main LV switchboards to distribution boards and process panels. Key features include internal arc protection (IEC TR 61641) and high-current busbar systems up to 13,000 A. The Logstrup Modular System supports scalability, retrofit, and future expansion with free selection of switchgear devices from leading OEMs.  With in-house manufacturing and a strong focus on quality, safety, and sustainability, Logstrup delivers reliable solutions for industries such as Energy, Datacenters, Infrastructure, Pharmaceutical, Marine/Offshore, NAVY, and Oil & Gas. profile_meta_description: Improve what moves. With this goal in mind, igus has been developing and producing motion plastics since 1964: lubrication-free, tribologically (i.e., in terms of friction and wear) optimized high-performance plastics for motion. These include products like energy chains, cables, bearings, linear and drive technology, semi-finished products, 3D printing, low-cost robotics, and intelligent sensor technology, which help customers improve their technology and reduce costs. Research in the company's own 5,500 sqm test laboratory continuously produces innovations and greater safety for users. At the Hannover Messe trade fair, igus will be showcasing new low-cost automation solutions that are intuitive to use, require no high investment, and do not involve complex integration. These range from humanoid robots starting at €5,000 and autonomous mobile robots to AI-based controls and the RBTX online platform, which is becoming a service hub for complete automation solutions.

profile_title: HANNOVER MESSE Exhibitor 2026: Aksa Process Engineering
profile_meta_description: Aksa Process Engineering designs and manufactures electromagnetic, magnetic, and physical separation technologies for industrial material processing. We develop integrated process systems covering comminution, classification, separation, thermal processing, and bulk material handling within a unified engineering framework. Our scope includes both standalone equipment and turnkey process plants, supported by process engineering, flowsheet development, commissioning, and technical services.  Our solutions are applied across mining, ceramics, iron & steel, recycling, industrial minerals, coal, and food industries. Systems are engineered for defined process conditions to ensure efficient separation, beneficiation, and impurity removal, with a focus on operational reliability and consistent product quality. We provide custom-engineered solutions tailored to specific material characteristics and process requirements.

profile_title: HANNOVER MESSE Exhibitor 2026: Ferespe Fundicao de Ferro e Aco
profile_meta_description: FERESPE is a sand and investment casting company producing technical parts for high-performance applications, whether for assembly lines or maintenance needs. As a bespoke steel foundry, we tailor our services to meet the specific requirements of each client, offering unique solutions that often involve specialized alloy compositions, testing, and certifications. With a strong focus on markets requiring cast components, particularly in the valve industry and sectors such as Energy, Oil & Gas, Clean & Renewable Energy, Water & Wastewater, LNG & Cryogenic, Refining & Petrochemical, Chemical, Railway, Pulp & Paper, Mining, Marine & Shipyard, we export about 90% of our production. Our cast components undergo heat treatment when required and are then processed in specialized workshops for machining, surface treatment, painting, and pre-assembly, before being delivered from our facilities - often directly to the customer’s production line.
